zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Clare於大約1091年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可克萊爾時，她的父親Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford27歲，母親Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont28歲。她於1111年與Geoffrey de St Leger在英國英格蘭素可費艾利特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1115年在英國享年25歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Clare于大约1091年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可克萊爾时，她的父亲Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford27岁，母亲Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont28岁。她于1111年与Geoffrey de St Leger在英国英格兰素可費艾利特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1115年在英国享年25岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Clare was born about 1091, in Clare, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, was 27 and her mother, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, was 28. She married Geoffrey de St Leger in 1111, in Fairlight, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in 1115, in England, at the age of 25.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Clare est née environ 1091, à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffrey de St Leger en 1111, à Fairlight, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée en 1115, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 25 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Clare um 1091 in Clare, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford 27 und ihre Mutter Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont 28. Sie hat 1111 in Fairlight, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Geoffrey de St Leger geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb 1115 im Alter von 25 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Clare è nata il circa 1091 nel Clare, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Geoffrey de St Leger nel 1111, nel Fairlight, Sussex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì nel 1115, nel England, all'età di 25 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Clare(이)가 대략 1091년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 클래르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1111년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 파어라이트에서 Geoffrey de St Leger(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1115년 영국에서 25세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Clare nasceu aproximadamente 1091, em Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Geoffrey de St Leger em 1111, em Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1115, em Inglaterra, com 25 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Clare родилась около 1091 г. в местности клар, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffrey de St Leger в 1111 г. в местности феирлаит, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в 1115 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 25 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Clare nació aproximadamente 1091, en Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Gilbert FitzRichard de Clare 1st Earl of Hertford, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Adelize en Beauvais de Clermont, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Geoffrey de St Leger en 1111, en Fairlight, Sussex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió en 1115, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 25 años.
